'''Albert Vete''' ({{langx|to|ʻAlipate Vete}}) (born 24 January 1993) is a [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has also spent time on loan from Castleford at [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

==Background==
Vete was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand. He is of [[Demographics of Tonga|Tongan]] descent.

Vete was educated at [[Saint Kentigern College]], playing [[rugby union]] and captaining their first XV to the Auckland title.<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.warriors.co.nz/team/profiles/albert-vete.html|title=Albert Vete|website=Warriors.co.nz|date=|accessdate=2015-12-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150730182109/http://www.warriors.co.nz/team/profiles/albert-vete.html|archive-date=2015-07-30|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early years===
Vete switched to rugby league in 2012, signing with the New Zealand Warriors and playing in their under 20's side while studying towards a Bachelor of Physical Education.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10770697|title=League: Warriors school up on union - Sport - NZ Herald News|website=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|date=2011-12-04|accessdate=2015-12-21}}</ref> Vete was part of the Junior Warriors side that played in [[2013 NRL Under-20s season|2013 Holden Cup]] Grand Final against the [[Penrith Panthers]] juniors, playing at prop and scoring a try in the 30–42 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/penrith-panthers-seal-thrilling-42-30-holden-cup-victory-over-new-zealand-warriors/story-fni3gfvk-1226733775052|title=Panthers seal Thrilling 42-30 Holden Cup Victory over New Zealand Warriors
|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|accessdate=2015-12-21}}</ref>

On 13 October 2013, Vete played for the [[Junior Kiwis]] against the [[Junior Kangaroos]] at prop in the Kiwis' 26–38 loss.<ref>{{cite web|author=Chris Barclay|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/9215327/Albert-Vete-chases-history-with-Junior-Warriors|title=Albert Vete chases history with Junior Warriors|website=Stuff.co.nz|date=2013-09-27|accessdate=2015-12-21}}</ref> In 2014 Vete joined the full-time squad and played in 19 games in the [[New South Wales Cup]] for the Warriors.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10885632|title=NRL: Five players earn extended Warriors' contracts - Sport - NZ Herald News|website=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|date=2013-05-23|accessdate=2015-12-21}}</ref>

===2015===
In Round 1 of the [[2015 NRL season]], Vete made his first grade [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the New Zealand Warriors against the [[Newcastle Knights]] off the interchange bench in the Warriors 24–14 loss at [[Hunter Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/66903948/New-faces-galore-for-New-Zealand-Warriors-clash-with-Newcastle-Knights|title=New faces galore for New Zealand Warriors' clash with Newcastle Knights|website=Stuff.co.nz|date=2015-03-03|accessdate=2015-12-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/knights-fight-back-to-beat-warriors/tabid/10874/newsid/84308/default.aspx|title=Knights fight back to beat Warriors|website=NRL.com|date=2015-03-07|accessdate=2015-12-21}}</ref>  On 2 May 2015, Vete played for Tonga against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the [[Polynesian Cup]], playing off the interchange bench in Tonga's 16–18 loss at [[Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/tonga-named-face-samoa-2015-pacific-test|title=Tonga Named To Face Samoa In Pacific Test|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2015-04-26|accessdate=2015-12-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203180119/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/tonga-named-face-samoa-2015-pacific-test/|archive-date=2016-02-03|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/video-sam-kasiano-try-saving-tackle-on-jorge-taufua-secures-pacific-test-win-for-samoan-against-tonga/story-e6frf3ou-1227331678892|title=Sam Kasiano Try Saving Tackle on Jorge Taufua Secures Pacific Test Win for Samoa Against Tonga|website=Foxsports.com.au|accessdate=2015-12-21}}</ref> On 7 July 2015, Vete re-signed with the Warriors on a 2-year contract to the end of the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/70027791/albert-vete-signs-twoyear-deal-with-warriors|title=Albert Vete signs two-year deal with Warriors|website=Stuff.co.nz|date=2015-07-07|accessdate=2015-12-21}}</ref> In Round 20 against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], Vete scored his first NRL career try in the Warriors 32–12 loss at [[Mt Smart Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/johnsons-season-over-as-manly-beat-warriors/tabid/10874/newsid/88343/default.aspx|title=Johnson's season over as Manly beat Warriors|website=NRL.com|date=2015-07-25|accessdate=2015-12-21}}</ref> Vete finished his debut year in the NRL with him playing in 21 matches and scoring 2 tries. On 17 October 2015, Vete played for Tonga in their [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification (Asia-Pacific play-off)|Asia-Pacific Qualifier]] match against the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.asiapacificrl.com/2015/10/09/tonga-finalise-squad-for-world-cup-qualifier/|title=Tonga finalise squad for World Cup Qualifier – Asia Pacific Rugby League Confederation|website=Asiapacificrl.com|date=|accessdate=2015-12-21|archive-date=22 December 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151222095333/http://www.asiapacificrl.com/2015/10/09/tonga-finalise-squad-for-world-cup-qualifier/|url-status=dead}}</ref> He played at prop in Tonga's 28–8 win at [[Campbelltown Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/tonga-qualify-for-2017-world-cup/tabid/10874/newsid/90566/default.aspx|title=Tonga qualify for 2017 World Cup|website=NRL.com|date=2015-10-17|accessdate=2015-12-21}}</ref>

===2016===
In February, Vete played for the Warriors in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/warriors-name-2016-nines-squad/|title=Warriors name 2016 Nines squad|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2016-01-29|accessdate=2016-02-04|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160204040722/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/warriors-name-2016-nines-squad/|archive-date=2016-02-04|url-status=dead}}</ref>

On 2 May, The New Zealand Herald reported five players were stood down from an international test match for mixing prescription drugs with energy drinks on a night out.  The recipe can emulate the effect of illicit drugs.  Vete was one of the players named who was involved in the activity.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-66-scandals-in-four-years-that-have-rocked-the-nrl/news-story/1bbcdffd432063bec8e521c736f7ae4d/|title=The 66 scandals in four years that have rocked the NRL|website=Fox Sports|date=8 February 2019 }}</ref>

===2018===
In July, Vete was a mid season trade from the [[New Zealand Warriors|Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/league-warriors-forward-albert-vete-picked-up-by-melbourne-storm/NQDWIMBE6HT7QELTM2YGP6GWVQ/ |title=League: Warriors forward Albert Vete picked up by Melbourne Storm |publisher=New Zealand Herald |date= |access-date=14 March 2023}}</ref> He made his [[Melbourne Storm]] debut against [[St George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]] in their round 17 clash.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2018/07/05/late-mail---round-17/ |title=Late Mail - Round 17 |publisher=Melbourne Storm |date=5 July 2018 |access-date=14 March 2023}}</ref>

===2019===
In the [[2019 NRL season]], Vete made only one appearance which came against [[St. George Illawarra]] in round 16 where Melbourne won 16–14.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2019/round-16/st-george-illawarra-vs-melbourne/stats.html |title=St George Illawarra Dragons 14 lost to Melbourne Storm 16 |publisher=Rugby League Project |access-date=14 March 2023}}</ref>

===2020===
Vete made only six appearances for Melbourne in the [[2020 NRL season]].  He did not play in the finals series or the [[2020 NRL Grand Final]] for Melbourne.  At the end of the season, he signed a contract to join [[Hull KR]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/albert-vete-hull-kr-debut-5211479/|title=Albert Vete gives verdict on Hull KR debut as he reveals role for round one|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

===2021===
In round 18 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Vete was controversially sent to the sin bin during Hull KR's 34–28 victory over [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58138787|title=Super League: Leigh Centurions 28-34 Hull Kingston Rovers - visitors edge 62-point thriller|work=BBC Sport }}</ref> In the elimination play off match against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], Vete was sent to the sin bin during Hull KR's 19–0 victory.  Vete made a total of 13 appearances including the club's semi-final defeat against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final|newspaper=The Guardian|date=30 September 2021 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58663060.amp/|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 0-19 Hull KR - Robins set up a semi-final at Catalans|website=www.bbc.com|date=23 September 2021 }}</ref>

===2022===
On 29 August, it was announced that Vete would be one of eleven players who were to depart [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at the end of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/hull-kr-confirm-11-departures-including-former-nrl-trio-and-star-winger-58198/|title=Hull KR confirm 11 departures including former NRL trio and star winger|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com|date=28 August 2022 }}</ref>
On 24 September, it was announced that he had signed a two-year deal to join [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].

===2023===
On 5 April 2023 it was reported that he would join [[Featherstone Rovers]] on a short-term loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/featherstone-rovers-bring-in-super-league-forward-on-loan|title=Featherstone Rovers bring in Super League duo on loan|publisher=Love Rugby League|accessdate=5 Apr 2023|date=5 Apr 2023}}</ref>

===2024===
On 19 Apr 24 [[Castleford Tigers]] confirmed that Vete had been released by the club, due to niggling injuries.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 April 2024|title=Castleford Tigers ‘actively looking’ to fill quota spot following Albert Vete release|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-actively-looking-to-fill-quota-spot-following-albert-vete-release}}</ref>
